block.description: BLOCK - Define a reusable template block. The block can be defined anywhere in the template and later called using PROCESS or INCLUDE. Blocks are not output where defined, only when invoked. Example: [% BLOCK header %]...[% END %] then [% PROCESS header %]

call.description: CALL - Evaluate an expression and discard the result. Useful for calling methods or functions for their side effects without outputting the return value. Example: [% CALL db.commit %] commits without printing the result

def.description: DEFAULT - Set a variable value only if it is undefined. Unlike SET, this preserves existing values. Useful for providing fallback values. Example: [% DEFAULT title = 'Untitled' %] sets title only if not already defined

end.description: END - Close a block directive. Required to terminate IF, FOREACH, WHILE, SWITCH, TRY, BLOCK, WRAPPER, FILTER, and MACRO blocks. Every opening block directive must have a matching END

for.description: FOREACH - Iterate over a list or hash. The loop variable ($item) contains each element. Use loop.first, loop.last, loop.size, loop.index, loop.count for metadata. Also supports FOREACH ... IN hash (iterates keys). Alias: FOR

get.description: GET - Retrieve and output the value of a variable or expression. This is the default behavior, so [% variable %] is equivalent to [% GET variable %]. Useful for explicit clarity or complex expressions

ifelse.description: IF/ELSE - Conditional block with alternative. Evaluates expression truthiness. Supports && (and), || (or), ! (not). The -%] chomps whitespace. Example: [% IF user.logged_in %]...[% ELSE %]...[% END %]

ifs.description: IF/ELSIF/ELSE - Multi-branch conditional. Evaluates conditions in order; first true branch executes. Use for multiple mutually exclusive conditions. Example: [% IF score >= 90 %]A[% ELSIF score >= 80 %]B[% ELSE %]C[% END %]

ifelsif.description: IF/ELSIF - Two-branch conditional without fallback. Use when you have specific conditions but no default case. Example: [% IF admin %]Admin Panel[% ELSIF moderator %]Mod Panel[% END %]

if.description: IF - Simple conditional block. Executes content only if expression is true. Supports comparison operators (==, !=, <, >, <=, >=) and logical operators (&&, ||, !). Example: [% IF items.size > 0 %]...[% END %]

ins.description: INSERT - Include a template file without processing. The file contents are inserted literally, no TT directives are evaluated. Faster than INCLUDE/PROCESS. Use for static content like copyright notices, disclaimers

inca.description: INCLUDE with args - Process a template with local variable assignments. Variables passed are local to the included template; changes don't affect caller. Example: [% INCLUDE header title='Page Title' theme='dark' %]

inc.description: INCLUDE - Process and include another template file. Creates a local scope; variable changes in the included template don't affect the caller. Template path is relative to INCLUDE_PATH config. Use for reusable components

macro.description:
MACRO - Define a reusable macro that can be called like a directive. Macros can accept arguments and contain complex logic. Example: [% MACRO header(title) BLOCK %]

[% title %]

[% END %] then use [% header('Welcome') %]

proca.description: PROCESS with args - Process a template with variable assignments. Unlike INCLUDE, PROCESS runs in the current scope and is faster. Variable changes persist after processing. Example: [% PROCESS component data=item %]

proc.description: PROCESS - Include and process another template in the current scope. Unlike INCLUDE, variable changes in the processed template affect the caller. More efficient than INCLUDE as it doesn't create a new scope. Use when sharing state is needed

set.description: SET - Assign a value to a variable. Can set multiple variables: [% SET x = 1, y = 2 %]. Use [% var = value %] as shorthand. Variables persist in the current scope. Use DEFAULT instead to avoid overwriting existing values

switch.description: SWITCH/CASE - Multi-way branch based on a value. More efficient than multiple IF/ELSIF for comparing one value against many options. CASE without a value is the default. Example: [% SWITCH status %][% CASE 'active' %]...[% CASE %]Unknown[% END %]

use.description: USE - Load and instantiate a Template Toolkit plugin. Plugins provide extended functionality like date formatting, database access, file operations. Example: [% USE Date %] then [% Date.format %]. Standard plugins: Date, Table, URL, HTML, DBI, XML

wrapa.description: WRAPPER with args - Wrap content with a template, passing arguments. The wrapper receives content via [% content %]. Variables passed become available in the wrapper. Example: [% WRAPPER layout title='Page' %]Main content here[% END %]

wrap.description: WRAPPER - Wrap content with another template. The wrapped content is passed to the wrapper as the 'content' variable. Use for layouts, frames, decorative borders. Example: WRAPPER layout.tt contains [% content %] placeholder where wrapped content appears
